vehicles.slot: JaguarDrive rotary dial — override near rotary base or under console trim
shift: Locate override slot near rotary dial base under console trim.
Insert key, press plunger.
Press brake. Rotate dial to N (or press N on newer electronic selector).
Confirm N on cluster.
neutral: Restore power. Shift via dial or override.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front (rear drive wheels roll). AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: AWD system: flatbed safest.
Rotary dial requires power — jump first if dead.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release. Manual: remove EPB module connector in trunk and connect spade connectors to motor pins to retract.
steps: Manual fallback: EPB module in trunk right side. Disconnect connector, insert spade connectors into correct slots (Violet/Brown and Yellow/Green pins noted on 2009-2015 XF). Connect to 12V to retract.

battery.warnings: AGM battery. AWD: flatbed preferred. EPB manual release requires wiring connector access.

knownIssues.title: EPB Manual Release Requires Wiring Access
knownIssues.detail: The manual release for the XF's electronic parking brake requires accessing the EPB motor connector in the trunk and connecting directly to the motor pins with spade connectors. Without power and without this method, the EPB cannot be released. A jump start is strongly preferred over the manual method.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector override near selector base under console trim
shift: Locate override near selector base.
Insert key, press.
Press brake. Move to N. Confirm.
neutral: Restore power. Shift normally or use override.
vehicles.notes: Compact sports sedan. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: AWD: flatbed.
All electronic — restore power first.

vehicles.slot: JaguarDrive rotary dial — override under console trim near dial base
shift: Locate override under rotary dial base in console.
Insert key, press plunger.
Press brake. Rotate dial or use button to select N.
Confirm N.
PHEV: restore 12V first.
neutral: Connect jump pack (12V only for PHEV).
Restore power. Shift via dial or override.
vehicles.notes: All F-Pace are AWD — FLATBED PREFERRED. PHEV: 12V only, flatbed only.
vehicles.warn: AWD: flatbed preferred. PHEV: 12V only, flatbed only.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED — AWD. PHEV: FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: All F-Pace are AWD — flatbed preferred.
PHEV: HV orange cables, flatbed mandatory.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button. Manual: EPB module connector in trunk — same method as XF.
steps: Manual fallback: EPB module connector in trunk.

towingNotes: Note vehicle height for loading clearance.

battery.warnings: AWD: flatbed preferred. PHEV: 12V only, flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — override near selector base under console trim
shift: Find override near selector base.
Insert key, press.
Press brake. Move to N. Confirm.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: rear lift. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: AWD: flatbed.

vehicles.slot: Electronic touchscreen — Service menu for Tow Mode / Neutral. Override near selector base.
shift: PREFERRED: Use touchscreen Service menu to select Tow Mode / Neutral.
Override: locate slot near selector base. Insert key, press. Press brake. Move to N.
Without power: connect 12V jump pack to under-hood or accessible terminals first.
neutral: 12V battery accessible under hood or in dedicated access point.
Restore power. Use Service menu for Tow Mode.
FLATBED ONLY. NEVER touch HV battery (orange cables).
vehicles.notes: FLATBED ONLY. Electric drivetrain. Tow Mode via touchscreen.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. 12V only — never touch HV orange cables/battery.
towNotes: Electric AWD — flatbed only.
Tow Mode via touchscreen is proper neutral method.
12V accessible under hood — check door jamb label for exact location.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Tow Mode on touchscreen releases EPB. Manual: EPB connector method (same as XF/F-Pace).
steps: Use Tow Mode on touchscreen — releases EPB and engages neutral.
Manual fallback: EPB module connector access.

battery.warnings: FLATBED ONLY. 12V auxiliary ONLY. Never touch orange HV cables.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — Electric AWD
knownIssues.detail: The I-Pace has permanent AWD electric motors. Towing with any wheels on the ground will damage the drive units. Flatbed with Tow Mode active is the only safe method.

vehicles.slot: Rotary or electronic selector override at selector base under console trim
shift: Locate override at selector base.
Insert key, press.
Press brake. Move to N. Confirm.
neutral: Restore power. Shift normally or use override.
vehicles.notes: Sports car. Very low clearance. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: Very low ground clearance. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front (rear drive wheels roll). AWD: flatbed preferred. Very low — use wheel nets, not chains on body.
towNotes: Very low ground clearance — check approach angle carefully.
AWD: flatbed.
Sports car — use wheel baskets on flatbed, never hook body.

battery.location: Under hood (front storage) or trunk depending on trim
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard or trunk area
battery.warnings: AGM battery. Very low clearance. AWD: flatbed preferred.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: The F-Type, especially with sport or lowered suspension, has very low ground clearance. The approach angle on a flatbed must be minimal. Use ramp extensions if available. Never chain or hook the body panels.

vehicles.slot: JaguarDrive rotary dial — override under console trim near dial base
shift: Override under rotary dial base.
Insert key, press plunger.
Press brake. Rotate dial to N.
Confirm N.
vehicles.notes: Flagship luxury sedan. Aluminum body — do NOT use standard chain/hook on body.
vehicles.warn: Aluminum body — do NOT hook or chain to body panels. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred. Aluminum body — use soft tie-downs and wheel baskets.
towNotes: Aluminum body is soft — do not hook body panels.
Use wheel baskets/straps on flatbed.
AWD: flatbed.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button. Manual: EPB module connector same as XF.

battery.warnings: Aluminum body — no chains on body. AGM battery. AWD: flatbed.

knownIssues.title: Aluminum Body — Do Not Hook or Chain
knownIssues.detail: The XJ uses an all-aluminum body structure. Standard chains or hooks on the body panels will cause serious damage. Always use wheel baskets/straps or hook only at proper tow points. Never drape chains over the body.

vehicles.slot: JaguarDrive rotary dial or J-gate lever — override slot at selector base under console trim
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
X300 (1995-1997): J-gate lever selector — locate override slot at selector base. Insert key, press firmly.
X308 (1997-2003): JaguarDrive rotary dial — locate slot near rotary base under console trim. Insert key, press.
While holding override, press the brake pedal.
Move selector or rotate dial to Neutral. Confirm N on cluster.
neutral: Battery under the hood — use AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
Aluminum body on X308 — do NOT hook or chain to body panels. Use wheel baskets or flatbed.
vehicles.notes: X308 has aluminum body — no chains on body. AGM battery.
vehicles.warn: Aluminum body — do NOT hook chains to body panels. Use wheel straps/baskets only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Flatbed preferred for aluminum-bodied X308.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Aluminum body — no chains on body panels. Flatbed preferred.

battery.warnings: AGM battery. Aluminum body — no chains on body.

knownIssues.title: Aluminum Body — No Chains on Body Panels
knownIssues.detail: The X308 XJ8 uses an aluminum body structure. Standard chains or hooks will cause serious damage. Always use wheel baskets or soft straps. Never drape chains over the body.

vehicles.trans: 3-speed automatic (GM TH400 early) or 4-speed automatic (GM 4L80E later)
vehicles.slot: Console-mounted selector — override slot or button near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the override slot or button near the selector base on the center console.
Insert key or flathead into slot and press firmly.
While holding, press brake pedal.
Move selector to Neutral.
neutral: Battery under the hood — connect jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
Very low, long vehicle — check clearance carefully. Flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.notes: Grand tourer. Very low clearance. Flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.warn: Extremely low ground clearance. Flatbed strongly preferred — minimal ramp angle required.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Flatbed strongly preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Very low clearance — flatbed strongly preferred.

battery.warnings: Very low clearance — flatbed strongly preferred.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: The XJ-S is a long, low grand tourer with very limited ground clearance. Flatbed loading requires a minimal approach angle. Use ramp extenders where available. Wheel-lift is possible but clearance is tight.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — in the TRUNK (boot), passenger side.
S-Type battery is in the trunk — do NOT look under the hood.
Once powered, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Battery in TRUNK. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: Battery is in the TRUNK — not under the hood.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
AWD: flatbed preferred.
Battery is in the TRUNK — open boot for jump access.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Trunk (Boot)
knownIssues.detail: The S-Type 12V battery is in the trunk (boot) on the passenger side. There is no battery under the hood. Always open the trunk to access jump terminals.

battery.warnings: Battery in trunk — not under the hood.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Ford Mondeo-based platform. AWD is standard on most US models — flatbed preferred. Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.warn: AWD standard on US models: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: AWD: flatbed preferred. FWD (rare): lift from front. AWD without flatbed: dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: AWD: flatbed preferred — full-time AWD cannot be easily disengaged.
FWD (rare): lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Connect jump pack to battery — battery location varies: X100 has battery in boot (trunk); X150 has a remote terminal under the hood.
X100 (1997-2006): BATTERY IN TRUNK (boot) — passenger side.
X150 (2007-2015): Jump terminals are under the hood — battery itself may be in the trunk.
Once powered, press brake and shift to Neutral.
vehicles.notes: X100 (1997-2006): battery in TRUNK. X150 (2007-2015): jump terminals under hood. High-value sports car.
vehicles.warn: Battery location varies by generation — see notes. High value — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. Flatbed strongly preferred due to vehicle value and low clearance.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
High-value vehicle — flatbed strongly preferred.
X100 battery in TRUNK. X150 jump terminals under hood.

knownIssues.title: Battery Location Varies by Generation
knownIssues.detail: X100 (1997-2006): 12V battery is in the trunk (boot). X150 (2007-2015): remote jump terminals are under the hood for convenience, but the battery may still be in the trunk. Always check under the hood first for the X150.

battery.location: X100: TRUNK (boot). X150: jump terminals under hood (battery in trunk on some trims).
battery.jumpTerminals: X100: trunk. X150: under hood remote terminals
battery.warnings: Battery or remote terminals location varies by generation.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Connect jump pack to battery — BATTERY IS IN THE TRUNK (boot).
Open the trunk and access the battery directly.
Once powered, press brake and shift to Neutral.
vehicles.notes: Battery in TRUNK. Aluminum body — do NOT use magnetic attachments or standard rubber-pad wheel restraints on exposed aluminum.
vehicles.warn: Battery in TRUNK. Aluminum body — avoid magnetic equipment.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear. AWD: flatbed preferred.
Battery in TRUNK — open boot for jump access.
Aluminum body — use non-magnetic equipment. Do not scratch painted surfaces with chain hooks.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Trunk
knownIssues.detail: The X350/X358 XJ 12V battery is in the trunk (boot). Open the trunk to access it.

battery.warnings: Battery in trunk. Aluminum body — use appropriate equipment.
